title: DNA Barcoding with qPCR to Support Food Authenticity Programs
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Food   authenticity     is     critical     to     protect     consumers     from     fraud  ,   ensure     compliance     with     labeling     regulations  ,   and     maintain     trust   in   the     food     supply     chain  . DNA   barcoding     combined     with     quantitative   PCR (  qPCR  )   offers     a     robust     approach     for     rapid  ,   sensitive  ,   and     specific     authentication     of     animal-derived     products  . By   amplifying     and     quantifying     species-specific   DNA,   qPCR     enables     accurate     identification     even   in   highly     processed     foods  .   This     molecular     approach     helps     detect     adulteration  ,   substitution  ,   or     mislabeling     of     meat  ,   dairy  ,   and     seafood     products   in real   time  .   Integrating     qPCR-based     barcoding     into     national     food     safety     programs     supports     surveillance  ,   strengthens     regulatory     enforcement  ,   and     minimizes     economic     losses     due     to     food     fraud  .   Adoption     of     this     technology     promotes     transparency  ,   enhances     traceability  ,   and     protects     both     consumer     health     and     fair     trade     practices     globally  .
